dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI had the least-bad day: Hacker News mocked its Trusted Access post as vague marketing, but that backlash was narrower than the stronger trust and security complaints aimed at Anthropic and Google Gemini [E3][E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won because its relevant signal was limited to skepticism about one announcement, while Anthropic absorbed a more concentrated hit over Claude Code trust, limits, and quality complaints, and Google Gemini drew repeated security, permissions, and workflow criticism across two threads [E3][E1][E2][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: HN reacted to OpenAI's Trusted Access announcement with sarcasm about marketing-heavy copy and a report that verification unlocked nothing in the API/Codex flow [E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code Routines drew distrust about platform lock-in, usage limits, and recent performance regressions, while a separate coding thread only added a mixed note that some developers are still leaning on Claude despite expecting risk [E1][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini-related discussion was dominated by worries about Chrome skill security, intrusive prompt history, weak permissions boundaries, and a separate complaint that managers use Gemini to validate poor ideas [E2][E4].

evidence.summary: On 'Claude Code Routines,' top comments questioned Anthropic's trustworthiness, worried about ToS and subscription constraints, cited recent usage-limit cuts, and said Claude had been performing badly.
Chrome's one-click AI prompt tools were discussed as Gemini-adjacent, with comments objecting to the security model, permissions, and Google inserting prompts into Gemini history.
OpenAI's 'Trusted access' announcement was met with sarcasm about vague cyber-marketing, comparisons to Anthropic's announcement, and one commenter saying verification unlocked nothing.
In a broader coding-responsibility thread, a commenter complained that managers have 'therapy sessions with Gemini' that validate bad ideas, adding a second negative Gemini signal.
The same coding thread included a developer saying they are increasingly offloading work to Claude despite admitting the practice adds risk, giving Anthropic a mixed secondary mention.
